title: Standpipe Operation — Engine Hose and Attack Hose
scene: Engine 181 is supplying a standpipe/FDC operation at a multi-story apartment building. A 100′ section of 2½″ hose runs from Engine 181 to the FDC. The standpipe carries water vertically to the fire floor. On the fire floor, a separate 2½″ attack hose is connected to the standpipe outlet and advanced to the nozzle.
studentQuestion: Calculate the pump pressure. Include the hose from Engine 181 to the FDC, standpipe system loss, elevation to the fire floor, the attack hose after the standpipe outlet, and nozzle pressure.
details: Attack hose after standpipe: 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the Floor 3 standpipe outlet
Nozzle after standpipe: 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 gpm
formulaBreakdown: Attack hose after standpipe = 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the Floor 3 outlet
Nozzle after standpipe = 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 gpm at 50 psi NP
PP = 35 FL + 50 NP + 15 elevation + 25 system loss
instructorExplanation: For this standpipe operation, calculate friction loss in two hose sections: the hose from Engine 181 to the FDC and the attack hose connected after the standpipe outlet. Then add nozzle pressure, elevation to the fire floor, and the 25 psi standpipe system loss.
explainMistake: Common mistakes are forgetting the Engine-to-FDC hose, only calculating the attack hose after the standpipe, forgetting the 25 psi standpipe system loss, using the wrong C value for 2½″ hose, using the wrong smooth bore flow, or missing the elevation pressure for the fire floor.

variations.change: Base question: 100′ of 2½″ hose from Engine 181 to the FDC, standpipe to Floor 3, then 150′ of 2½″ attack hose with a 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle.
variations.question: Engine 181 is supplying the FDC with 100′ of 2½″ hose. The standpipe rises to Floor 3. A 150′ section of 2½″ attack hose is connected to the 3rd floor standpipe outlet and ends in a 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle. What pump pressure should you set?
details: Attack hose after standpipe: 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the Floor 3 standpipe outlet
Nozzle after standpipe: 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 gpm
formulaBreakdown: Attack hose after standpipe = 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the Floor 3 outlet
Nozzle after standpipe = 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 gpm at 50 psi NP
PP = 35 FL + 50 NP + 15 elevation + 25 system loss
variations.instructorExplanation: Start with the hose from Engine 181 to the FDC, then add the standpipe system loss, elevation to the fire floor, the attack hose after the standpipe outlet, and the nozzle pressure. Both hose sections use the listed length and size.

variations.change: Increase the attack hose after the standpipe outlet to 200′. Keep 100′ of 2½″ hose from Engine 181 to the FDC, the Floor 3 outlet, and the 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle.
variations.question: Engine 181 is supplying the FDC with 100′ of 2½″ hose. The standpipe rises to Floor 3. A 200′ section of 2½″ attack hose is connected to the 3rd floor standpipe outlet and ends in a 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle. What pump pressure should you set?
details: Attack hose after standpipe: 200′ of 2½″ hose connected at the Floor 3 standpipe outlet
Nozzle after standpipe: 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 gpm
formulaBreakdown: Attack hose after standpipe = 200′ of 2½″ hose connected at the Floor 3 outlet

PP = 42 FL + 50 NP + 15 elevation + 25 system loss
